verdict_reasoning: C3 is a secreted plasma glycoprotein (no TM domain, GPI anchor, or membrane-anchoring mechanism) produced primarily by hepatocytes and macrophages and released into circulation. Upon activation by C3 convertases, C3b exposes a reactive thioester with a ~100 µs half-life that can covalently deposit onto hydroxyl/amino groups of any nearby surface (pathogens, apoptotic cells, immune complexes, or bystander host cells). This is opsonization chemistry — a circulating secreted protein nonspecifically cross-linking to extracellular surfaces — equivalent to matrix/stroma deposition, which per triage rules stays in 'secreted_only'. C3b's deposition is not a stable partnership with a defined TM-anchored partner, and C3 itself has no PM-trafficking relationship via its own anchor. Pre-no checklist: (1) no cell-surface-directed therapeutic targets C3 on a host cell surface (anti-C3 programs target the circulating or deposited opsonin); (2) no TM precursor / sheddase biology; (3) no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to a defined surface scaffold (thioester-to-carbohydrate/protein is nonspecific opsonization, not a stable_surface_attachment to a TM partner); (4) no documented PM minority pool on the C3-producing cell; (5) no cell-state/cancer/apoptosis/activation ecto-pool on outer leaflet of expressing cells documented with gene-specific surface assays.
